notes: The month's choices. Each activity spends the month: it moves aptitudes (hands/sea/word/heart/wild/grit), maybe a bond, maybe money, maybe adds a journal entry. 'months' (0=Sep .. 9=Jun) gates a seasonal activity; absent = any month. 'require' gates on an aptitude min, a bond min, or money. March (index 6) is handled specially by the scene (the water comes) and does not draw from this pool.

activities.label: Mend nets on the dock with Del
activities.journal: A net is a year of small repairs. You can read a man's winter in his mending.
activities.outcome: Del works in silence and you learn to work in it too.

activities.label: Take a shift on the cannery line
activities.outcome: Cold, wet, loud, and by the end your hands know the knife.

activities.label: Spend the afternoon at the library
activities.journal: The tide tables are a book that tells the future, and nobody calls it that.
activities.outcome: Iris slides you the book she thinks you actually want.

activities.label: Wash dishes for Ruth at the cafe
activities.outcome: You hear half the town's news over the sink and keep all of it.

activities.label: Dig razor clams on the minus tide
activities.journal: Razor clams go down faster than you can dig. The tide is the only clock that matters.
activities.outcome: Freezing, filthy, a full bucket. A good morning.

activities.label: Pick salmonberries up the draw
activities.journal: Salmonberry first, then thimbleberry, then blackberry — the summer keeps a calendar in fruit.
activities.outcome: You eat as many as you sell. Boyd eats more.

activities.label: Walk the beach and see what came in
activities.journal: Sanderlings run the wave-edge like they are sewing it shut.
activities.outcome: A glass float, a gull's wing, a boot. The beach gives what it gives.

activities.label: Help Vovo in the garden
activities.journal: The grandmother grows things that are not from here — kale that is a tree, a bean nobody names in English.
activities.outcome: She corrects your hands without a word and you feel it as kindness.

activities.label: Sit with Vovo while she sings
activities.journal: The songs are older than the town and came across an ocean to get here.
activities.outcome: You catch a little more of it each time. It is starting to stay in you.

activities.label: Just go to school
activities.outcome: You are the new kid until, one ordinary Tuesday, you are not.

activities.label: Take the skiff out past the jetty
activities.journal: At slack water the bar lies flat and lies about what it is.
activities.outcome: You cross the bar and come back in and Del does not say well done, which means well done.

activities.label: Go up into the woods with Boyd
activities.journal: Elk sign, bear scat, a spring nobody marked. The woods are a town too.
activities.outcome: Boyd shows you the coast from the bluff. You do not talk for a while.

activities.label: Run the delivery route for Tuck
activities.outcome: Every porch is a person. By the end you know the town by its doors.

activities.label: Bring in Estelle's wood
activities.outcome: She keeps a light in the window that faces the bar. You do not ask why. You are starting to know.

activities.label: Watch the storm come in from the point
activities.journal: When the glass falls fast, the town brings the boats in and nobody has to be told.
activities.outcome: The whole coast goes the color of a bruise and then it hits.

activities.label: Go to the church supper
activities.outcome: The whole town in one warm room. Even Estelle comes, and sits at the back, and stays.

activities.label: Listen for the fleet on Del's boathouse radio
activities.journal: Every boat has a call sign and a voice. You learn to tell them apart in the static.
activities.outcome: Del lets you keep the watch while he mends. You listen for the ones that answer, and notice the one channel that never does.

activities.label: Read the county register with Iris
activities.journal: The town keeps two histories: the one they printed, and the one in Iris's back room.
activities.outcome: Iris turns the ledger to a page she has clearly turned to before, and lets you read it, and says nothing.
